_comment: The one file you tune per project. /harness-init writes the stack-specific bits. A project is one-or-more COMPONENTS (each a buildable sub-repo with its own stack + gate) plus a cross-cutting root gate for integration/e2e. A single-root project is just one component with path '.'. See harness.schema.json for the full field reference.

project._comment: greenfield = building from scratch (establish stack/specs, higher autonomy OK). brownfield = an existing codebase (understand & respect it first, supervised by default, baseline must be green before changes). /harness-init detects and sets this.

models._comment: Per-phase model routing (declared here; enforced via settings.json `model`, agent frontmatter, and loop --model flags — /harness-doctor checks they agree). Each phase is {model, fallback}: model/fallback are a Claude alias|full ID or the literal 'codex' (cross-vendor OpenAI Codex CLI); null = inherit session / no fallback. fallback fires when the primary is unavailable or hits a usage/limit error (never codex->codex). codex{} configures the Codex CLI for ANY phase routed to it. The legacy flat 'phase': 'alias' form is still accepted by the resolver for back-compat.

parallel._comment: Fleet runner (harness/fleet.ps1|.sh): opt-in parallel workers over independent tasks (declared 'files' ownership in tasks.json), serialized merge queue. 3 = the productive default; review bandwidth binds before compute.

gate._comment: Cross-cutting / integration gate, run from the repo ROOT after every component gate passes. For a multi-component project this usually holds only the e2e that exercises the components together; leave the rest null. For a single-component project, leave this entirely null (the component gate covers it).
